summary: The main claim is: But I think it's likely we'll see AGI with all of the agency that humans have.
When someone refers to AI as a technology, I think they're often not thinking of it as having full agency.

The main claim is: Mechanistic interpretability that only statically analyzes a finished model proceeds 'in the dark'; incorporating the causal formation of features across the whole training process should make interpretability easier and more holistic.

Training-process transparency may be the only way to catch some dangerous failures — planted backdoors, gradient hacking, deceptive alignment — that no efficient distinguisher could detect in the final model alone.
